behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between manifest validation and plugin loading/execution. A unit test of only the validator could pass while the loader still imports the plugin module too early.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with a malformed `manifest.json` and an entry module that writes a sentinel file (or flips shared state) on import side effect. Run the real loader against that directory and assert: (1) loader reports manifest validation failure, and (2) sentinel side effect never occurs.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: This depends on resolver + scheduler + hook invocation across multiple plugins. Single-function unit tests of topological sort may miss wiring bugs in actual load execution order.
behaviors.test_description: Build two real test plugins in a temp directory (`A` depends on `B`), each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory array exposed by the test harness. Execute loader end-to-end and assert call order is `["B","A"]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection alone can be unit-tested, but this behavior also requires proving neither plugin is loaded/invoked by the real loader pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins `A` and `B` with mutual dependencies and import/onLoad side effects (e.g., append to log). Run loader and assert: (1) cycle error contains both plugin names, (2) no import side effects observed, and (3) neither `onLoad` ran.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: The bug appears at the handoff between module import lifecycle and hook invocation. You need real module execution ordering, not just isolated hook-call logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets `globalThis.pluginReady = true` during top-level execution; its `onLoad` throws if that flag is absent. Run loader and assert `onLoad` succeeds and observes initialized state, proving import execution preceded hook call.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Requires graph-aware continuation semantics across multiple plugins plus error isolation. Unit tests of try/catch or dependency filtering alone can miss real orchestration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins: `Failing` throws in `onLoad`; `Independent` has no dependency on `Failing`; `Dependent` depends on `Failing`. Run loader and assert: (1) `Failing` error is reported, (2) `Independent.onLoad` still runs, (3) `Dependent.onLoad` does not run.
